title: quassia multiflora
intro: Quassia multiflora, also known as the "Yellow Dogwood" or "Quassia Tree", is a tropical plant native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the family Rutaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, that are believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and supporting immune system function. Herbal preparations from Quassia multiflora can be found in traditional medicine, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
